lead: This week in tech highlights significant advancements in AI applications, startup successes, and the evolving landscape of technology in Australia.

stories.title: OpenAI just turned ChatGPT into the backend for the most popular open-source project in history.
stories.summary: OpenAI has integrated its ChatGPT subscription service into OpenClaw, a leading open-source project, allowing users to leverage their ChatGPT accounts for enhanced functionality. This move signifies a major shift in how AI tools can be utilized within open-source frameworks, potentially increasing user engagement and project scalability.

stories.title: Melbourne Airport calls in AI agents for incident response.
stories.summary: In a bid to enhance operational efficiency, Melbourne Airport has deployed AI agents to assist in incident response. These AI tools are designed to streamline the checking of Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and facilitate report generation, showcasing a practical application of AI in improving safety and response times in critical environments.

stories.title: Skio never hired a sales team, never ran an ad, and just sold for $105 million cash to the company it was built to replace.
stories.summary: Startup Skio, which revolutionized subscription payments without traditional marketing strategies, has been acquired for $105 million. This remarkable exit illustrates the potential for innovative solutions in the e-commerce space, particularly as businesses seek efficient payment systems.

stories.title: AI tool ‘too dangerous to release’ could wreak havoc on businesses.
stories.summary: A new AI tool developed by Anthropic has raised alarms due to its potential to exploit vulnerabilities in widely-used software. Experts warn that its release could lead to significant cybersecurity breaches, highlighting the dual-edged nature of AI advancements.

stories.title: In Harvard study, AI offered more accurate emergency room diagnoses than two human doctors.
stories.summary: A recent study from Harvard University found that AI models can provide more accurate diagnoses in emergency room settings compared to human doctors. This breakthrough underscores the potential of AI to enhance medical decision-making and patient outcomes.

stories.title: Amazon’s best quarter in years was half-built on a $16.8 billion paper gain from Anthropic.
stories.summary: Amazon reported a record quarterly revenue, significantly boosted by its investment in Anthropic. This financial success highlights the growing impact of AI investments on traditional business models and the tech industry's economic landscape.

stories.title: Every AI company now wants to ride shotgun.
stories.summary: The integration of AI into car dashboards is becoming a hot trend, with applications like Grok and ChatGPT enhancing user experience. This shift indicates a growing demand for AI-driven interfaces in vehicles, transforming how drivers interact with technology on the road.

stories.title: Tesla is selling Chinese-made cars in Canada to escape the tariffs.
stories.summary: Tesla has started selling Chinese-made Model 3 sedans in Canada, strategically navigating tariffs imposed by both China and the US. This move not only reduces costs for consumers but also reflects Tesla's adaptability in a complex global market.

stories.title: Planet Labs is not selling satellite images; it is selling a subscription to watch the entire planet change in real time.
stories.summary: Planet Labs has launched a subscription service that allows users to monitor global changes through satellite imagery. This innovative approach to earth observation represents a significant advancement in how we can track environmental and societal changes over time.
